title: Stack overflow while parsing deeply nested JSON
poc:
WITH RECURSIVE c(x) AS (VALUES(1) UNION ALL SELECT x+1 FROM c WHERE x<400000)
SELECT json_valid(group_concat('[', '')) FROM c;
description:
drh added on 2017-04-11 18:09:39:
The following query causes a stack overflow in the JSON parser:
WITH RECURSIVE c(x) AS (VALUES(1) UNION ALL SELECT x+1 FROM c WHERE x<400000)
SELECT json_valid(group_concat('[', '')) FROM c;
This problem was reported on the SQLite mailing list by Ralf Junker and is
based on the n_structure_100000_opening_arrays.json test case from the
JSONTestSuite.
key_bug_features: Recursive CTE with large depth causes stack overflow
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT, depth INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0);
WITH RECURSIVE c(x) AS (VALUES(1) UNION ALL SELECT x+1 FROM c WHERE x<400000) SELECT json_valid(group_concat('[', '')) FROM c;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE depth IS NULL;
UPDATE test_table SET value = 'Updated' WHERE id = (SELECT id FROM test_table ORDER BY depth DESC LIMIT 1);
